Most Americans Say “Not in My Backyard” to AI Data Centers

Published on July 22nd, 2026 by Dana Anderson

  • 53% of U.S. residents oppose building a data center in their area, while 34% support it.
  • Data center hubs have seen education spending nearly double over the last decade.

More than half of U.S. residents oppose the construction of an AI data center in their neighborhood, according to a recent survey. Roughly one-third support it.

The survey included four thousand residents and measured whether respondents supported or opposed several kinds of construction in their neighborhoods.

AI Data Centers Are Helping Fund Virginia Schools

The boom in AI data centers is helping fund public schools through a surge in tax revenue. The largest data-center hubs have seen faster education-spending growth than neighboring counties.

Impact of AI Data Centers on Education Spending

Select Northern Virginia counties

Loudoun County, VAPrince William County, VAFairfax County, VAStafford County, VA
Number of data centers176

(most in the U.S.)

77451
Increase in personal property tax revenue, 2010-2025639%349%91%
Increase in education spending per resident, 2010-202577%82%49%29%

Methodology

The report combines county financial records with population estimates to calculate per-resident figures and growth rates.

Dana Anderson

As a data journalist at Redfin, Dana writes about the numbers behind real estate trends.
